assumptions: The developer has a supported browser, a phone number, and a credit or debit card for identity verification.
The developer can use a Microsoft account or GitHub account identity.
No Azure subscription, sample deployment, or developer-tool environment exists yet.

developer_goal: Create an Azure account and reach the earliest meaningful Azure developer first-success outcome.

first_success.outcome: Unresolved: the developer-intro quickstart ends after deploying a sample app, inspecting resources, and using GitHub Copilot for Azure, while other official portals offer different first resources.
first_success.observable_signal: Candidates include a deployed to-do app URL, portal Quickstart Center completion, or a portal VM.

route_selection.surface: Azure free account signup and Azure developer documentation
route_selection.rule: Prefer the vendor developer-intro quickstart only if Copilot and tool prerequisites are accepted as part of the same first success; otherwise treat portal and azd-only routes as consequential alternatives.

candidates.name: Quickstart: Get started with Azure developer tools
candidates.reason: Single quickstart under Introduction to Azure for developers, but terminal includes GitHub Copilot for Azure and a Copilot subscription prerequisite.

candidates.name: Browser VS Code for the Web versus local tool install
candidates.reason: The same quickstart offers two environment branches with different install burden before azd deploy.

candidates.name: Azure Quickstart Center or portal VM quickstart
candidates.reason: Official portal routes reach different environment-setup or VM outcomes and are not the developer-intro quickstart.

required_path.action: Sign up for an Azure free account or pay-as-you-go account using a Microsoft or GitHub identity.

required_fields.label: Microsoft account or GitHub account
required_fields.notes: FAQ states either identity works; live form controls after identity sign-in were not fully observed.

required_fields.label: Credit or debit card
required_fields.notes: Used for identity verification; temporary authorization may appear.

required_path.observable_result: An Azure subscription is available to the signed-in identity.

required_path.action: Choose the first-success route: developer-tools quickstart, Quickstart Center, or another portal product quickstart.
required_path.observable_result: One bounded Azure onboarding path is fixed.

required_path.action: If using the developer-tools quickstart, open VS Code for the Web or a local environment and complete the selected tool setup.

required_fields.label: GitHub Copilot subscription for Copilot-for-Azure terminal
required_fields.notes: Required for the guide's stated Copilot hands-on terminal; may be out of scope if first success stops at deploy verification.

required_path.observable_result: The chosen Azure developer tool environment can authenticate and run azd.

prerequisites.description: Phone number plus credit or debit card for Azure account identity verification.
For the developer-tools quickstart Copilot exercise, a GitHub Copilot subscription.

external_gates.description: Payment-method identity verification with a possible temporary authorization hold.
GitHub authorization when signing up or signing in with GitHub.

unavoidable_waits.description: azd up provisioning and deployment of the sample App Service and Cosmos DB resources before the app URL can be opened.

platform_outcomes.description: Azure free account signup grants $200 credit for 30 days and free-tier monthly amounts when eligibility checks pass.
azd up creates the resource group, provisions services, and deploys the sample application for the developer-tools quickstart.

excluded.item: Reading Introduction to Azure for developers
excluded.reason: Documentation reading is not a counted product action.

excluded.item: Next steps and cleanup after the developer-tools quickstart
excluded.reason: Official guide places cleanup after the demonstrated terminal exercises.

sources.title: Introduction to Azure for developers
evidence_supported: Single Quickstart link under Get started

sources.title: Quickstart: Get started with Azure developer tools
evidence_supported: azd init and azd up sample deploy

sources.title: Get started with the Azure Quickstart Center
Create Your Azure Free Account Or Pay As You Go

uncertainties.question: Does first success stop when the deployed to-do URL works, or must it include Copilot for Azure as the guide's stated terminal?
uncertainties.impact: Including Copilot adds a paid GitHub Copilot subscription gate and extra IDE actions.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Human decision on the measurement boundary, then re-audit the selected terminal against the current quickstart.

uncertainties.question: What exact signup form fields appear after Microsoft or GitHub sign-in on signup.azure.com?
uncertainties.impact: Account field counts remain partly FAQ-level rather than observed-public-ui.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Dated screenshots of the live Azure free-account signup wizard for a new identity.
